nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated species occurring around tropical atolls and lagoons at depths of roughly 0–30 m; juveniles have been reported to associate with sea anemones and coral heads in a manner similar to the closely related Threespot Dascyllus

diet.notes: Generally accepts a wide range of prepared foods including marine flakes and pellets, frozen mysis and brine shrimp, finely chopped seafood, and herbivore preparations with spirulina. Wild-caught specimens have been reported to feed primarily on zooplankton and small benthic invertebrates while also grazing on filamentous algae.

avoidWith: conspecifics in tanks below roughly 400 L

summary: The Golden Domino Damselfish (Dascyllus auripinnis) is a hardy black-bodied reef fish from the Eastern Central Pacific whose yellow belly and golden fins distinguish it from its all-black Threespot Dascyllus relative. Tolerant of imperfect water but tends to grow highly territorial.
careNotes:
Dascyllus auripinnis was described in 2001 by Randall & Randall and is the close yellow-bellied congener of the much better-known Threespot Damselfish (Dascyllus trimaculatus). Juveniles display three bright white spots on a dark body, very similar to the Threespot, but D. auripinnis develops golden-yellow coloration on the lower body, pelvic, anal, and caudal fins as it matures. Trade specimens are sometimes labelled 'Dascyllus cf auripinnis' because Fijian imports may represent a closely-related undescribed form rather than the strictly-defined type-locality species from Phoenix/Line/Cook Islands.

This species is commonly recommended to beginning saltwater hobbyists because it has been reported to tolerate substandard water parameters and is generally easy to feed. However, beginners should be aware that what starts as a small, sociable juvenile typically matures into a highly territorial and aggressive adult. It has been known to chase and nip at smaller or more docile tankmates and is generally considered incompatible with peaceful species such as fairy wrasses, gobies, blennies, and clownfish once established.

Sources disagree on minimum tank size: LiveAquaria and BlueZoo cite a 30-gallon (~114 L) minimum, ThatPetPlace recommends 55 gallons (~208 L), and Reeflex suggests up to 1500 L for adults. A minimum of 200 L (about 55 gal) was selected here to provide enough territory for a single adult and reduce stress on tankmates — keeping multiple individuals together has been reported to require considerably more volume.

Water parameters cross-checked: LiveAquaria gives temperature 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C), pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12; ThatPetPlace gives 75–82°F (23.9–27.8°C), pH 8.0–8.4, SG 1.020–1.024; Reeflex gives 20–28°C. Averaged values (23–27°C, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025) are used here. Maximum size is reported at 4.5 in (~11.4 cm) by LiveAquaria/BlueZoo/ThatPetPlace and 11.5 cm SL by FishBase; Reeflex's 15 cm TL appears high. A range of roughly 8–12 cm captures the realistic adult size in captivity.

The species is considered reef-safe in the sense that it does not consume corals or clean-up-crew invertebrates, but its aggressive territoriality can disrupt the peace of a community reef. Like the Threespot Dascyllus, juveniles have been reported to associate facultatively with large host anemones such as carpet anemones (Stichodactyla spp.) when one is available.
breedingNotes: Dascyllus auripinnis is oviparous with distinct pairing during breeding; males clear a flat substrate site, then guard and aerate the demersal egg mass until hatching. Captive breeding has not been widely documented in the hobby and aquacultured offspring are not currently available in the trade; specimens are typically wild-caught from Fiji and surrounding reefs.

primary.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
primary.notes: Used as primary because the assigned LiveAquaria collection URL was a category page and did not directly cover this species. Provided scientific name, common-name synonyms, max size (4.5 in), minimum tank (30 gal), temperament (semi-aggressive), care level (beginner), diet (omnivore), origin (Indo-Pacific, Fiji), and reef compatibility (yes).

additional.name: LiveAquaria
additional.notes: Direct species product page (located via web search since the supplied collection URL did not list this species directly). Provided care level (Easy), temperament (Aggressive, territorial with age), max size (~4.5 in), family (Pomacentridae), temperature (72-78°F / 22.2-25.6°C), pH (8.1-8.4), specific gravity (1.020-1.025), dKH (8-12), minimum tank (30 gal / ~114 L), diet, and reef-safe (yes). Note the scientific name was hedged as 'Dascyllus cf auripinnis'.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Randall & Randall, 2001), family (Pomacentridae), order (Ovalentaria/misc), maximum size (11.5 cm SL), Eastern Central Pacific distribution (Phoenix, Line, and Cook Islands), depth 0-30 m, oviparous reproduction with male egg-guarding, and IUCN Least Concern status.

additional.name: ThatPetPlace
additional.notes: Cross-checked minimum tank (55 gal / ~208 L), temperature (75-82°F / 23.9-27.8°C), pH (8.0-8.4), specific gravity (1.020-1.024), omnivorous diet, and territorial temperament. Drove the conservative-larger bias toward 200 L over LiveAquaria's 114 L.

additional.name: Reeflex
additional.notes: Cross-checked Central Pacific distribution (Kiribati, Cook Islands, Line Islands, Phoenix Islands), depth 1-30 m, temperature 20-28°C, and territorial aggression. Reeflex's 1500 L minimum tank recommendation is an outlier (designed for adult colony rather than single specimen) and was not used as the captive minimum.

fish.breedingNotes: Spawns oviparously in the wild with demersal eggs guarded by the male; captive breeding has not been widely documented for hobbyists and aquacultured juveniles are not generally available.
